vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The visual data indicates a catastrophic failure of the expected biome. The registry lists P1, P2, P3, and P4, but the current visual field contains only two visible pot structures (one yellow, one black) and no identifiable botanical specimens.

inventory_reconciliation.P3_Pothos: Systemic Loss (White rabbit anchor is absent/not visible)

plant_audit.notes: The pots appear to contain only substrate/soil. No foliage, stems, or root structures are detectable in any of the provided images.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Dark, damp-appearing substrate with no signs of vegetative growth.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of a white cylindrical object (likely a sensor or container) and a spoon in the yellow pot. No fungal or moss growth detected.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_current: The sequence shows a static, empty environment. There is no evidence of plant life in the earliest image, nor any development in the subsequent frames. The environment is effectively a 'dead' biome.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently non-functional. The absence of the P3 anchor (white rabbit) and the lack of any green matter suggests either a complete removal of specimens or a failure to establish the biome entirely.
anomalies: Presence of a spoon in the yellow pot (P1 location).
Presence of a white cylindrical object in the yellow pot.
Total absence of the P3 scale anchor (white rabbit).
vision_report.narrative_description: My audit confirms a total systemic loss of the registered botanical collection. Across the five-day sequence, the pots remain devoid of plant life. The presence of non-botanical items (spoon, cylinder) suggests the pots are being utilized for non-horticultural purposes or have been completely cleared of their intended occupants. No growth, decay, or change in soil composition was observed, indicating a static, inactive state.
